techniques.evidence_quote: A firewall misconfiguration permitted commands to reach and be executed by that server, which enabled access to folders or buckets
aws iam list-roles ... aws iam list-policies ... aws iam list-instance-profiles
a list of more than 700 folders or buckets of data

techniques.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.evidence_quote: curl http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ ... This command retrieves the current IAM temporary credentials from the role from the EC2 instance metadata.
obtained the security credentials for an account known as ***-WAF-Role that, in turn, enabled access to certain of Capital One's folders
the third command (the "Sync Command"), when executed, used the ***-WAF-Role to extract or copy data from those folders or buckets
the attacker used Tor and IPredator in combination to obfuscate her identity

attack_chain.description: Attacker discovered a misconfigured firewall (likely Security Group/WAF) in the Capital One AWS environment, permitting commands to reach and be executed by a server, resulting in SSRF/command execution on an EC2 instance.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: A firewall misconfiguration permitted commands to reach and be executed by that server, which enabled access to folders or buckets

evidence_chunks.quote: A firewall misconfiguration permitted commands to reach and be executed by that server, which enabled access to folders or buckets
A firewall misconfiguration permitted commands to reach and be executed by that server, which enabled access to folders or buckets

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_chain.description: Attacker queried the EC2 Instance Metadata Service (IMDS at 169.254.169.254) to obtain temporary IAM credentials for the ***-WAF-Role attached to the compromised EC2 instance.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the first command, when executed, obtained the security credentials for an account known as ***-WAF-Role

evidence_chunks.quote: the first command, when executed, obtained the security credentials for an account known as ***-WAF-Role
the first command, when executed, obtained the security credentials for an account known as ***-WAF-Role

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the stolen ***-WAF-Role IAM temporary credentials to authenticate to AWS APIs with the over-privileged role's permissions.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: obtained the security credentials for an account known as ***-WAF-Role that, in turn, enabled access to certain of Capital One's folders

evidence_chunks.quote: obtained the security credentials for an account known as ***-WAF-Role that, in turn, enabled access to certain of Capital One's folders
obtained the security credentials for an account known as ***-WAF-Role that, in turn, enabled access to certain of Capital One's folders

attack_chain.description: Using the assumed role, the attacker enumerated S3 buckets and folders accessible to the role, identifying more than 700 folders/buckets of data.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a list of more than 700 folders or buckets of data

evidence_chunks.quote: a list of more than 700 folders or buckets of data
a list of more than 700 folders or buckets of data

attack_chain.description: Attacker executed the 'Sync Command' (aws s3 sync) with the ***-WAF-Role credentials to copy/exfiltrate data from S3 buckets containing ~100 million credit application records.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the third command (the "Sync Command"), when executed, used the ***-WAF-Role to extract or copy data from those folders or buckets

evidence_chunks.quote: the third command (the "Sync Command"), when executed, used the ***-WAF-Role to extract or copy data from those folders or buckets
the third command (the "Sync Command"), when executed, used the ***-WAF-Role to extract or copy data from those folders or buckets

initial_access_vector: Misconfigured WAF/firewall allowed SSRF-style command execution against an EC2-hosted server, enabling the attacker to interact with the EC2 Instance Metadata Service.
privilege_escalation: Use of an over-privileged EC2 IAM role (***-WAF-Role) whose credentials were retrieved via IMDS; the role had broad S3 list/read permissions that the compromised server should not have had.
persistence_mechanism: None documented; attacker relied on temporary IAM credentials obtained via IMDS rather than establishing persistence.
